scenario: Checklist items require link + page/section/paragraph but the customer used inconsistent formats.
allowed_transformations: Normalize evidence pointers into a consistent format: "Doc: <name>, Page: <n>, Section: <title>, Paragraph: <n>"
Fix grammar and punctuation without changing meaning
forbidden_transformations: Claim evidence exists if it does not
placeholder_policy: If a pointer is missing, insert: "MISSING EVIDENCE: Evidence pointer (doc/page/section/paragraph). This was not found in the uploaded files. Add it (or upload a document) before claiming FTR readiness."
example_safe_output: Doc: Deployment Guide, Page: [TBD], Section: [TBD], Paragraph: [TBD]. MISSING EVIDENCE: Evidence pointer (doc/page/section/ paragraph). This was not found in the uploaded files. Add it (or upload a document) before claiming FTR readiness.
reviewer_warning: Do not guess references. If missing, use placeholders and mark as incomplete.

scenario: Docs contain credential-like strings in examples (keys/tokens/ passwords).
allowed_transformations: Add an explicit warning: "Do not hardcode credentials"
forbidden_transformations: Change authentication approach (e.g., switching to roles) unless the docs already describe it
placeholder_policy: If the correct secure mechanism is not described, add: "MISSING EVIDENCE: Secure credential delivery mechanism. This was not found in the uploaded files. Add it (or upload a document) before claiming FTR readiness."
example_safe_output:
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<REDACTED_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID>
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<REDACTED_AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY>
Do not hardcode credentials. Use IAM roles or a centralized secret store.
reviewer_warning: If real secrets were present, that is a security incident. Rotate immediately; do not store/echo them.

scenario: Incident plan exists but is a wall of text with unclear roles/ steps.
allowed_transformations: Reformat into headings and bullet steps
Extract existing role names and place under 'Roles/On-call'
forbidden_transformations: Invent escalation contacts, phone numbers, or tools
Claim on-call coverage if not stated
placeholder_policy: Insert placeholders using the standard 'MISSING EVIDENCE' string for missing roles/steps/maintenance date.
example_bad_input: We handle incidents quickly and notify customers as needed.
example_safe_output:
## Incident Management Plan
- Severity levels: [TBD]
- Roles/on-call: [TBD]
- Triage steps: [TBD]
- Customer communication: [TBD]
MISSING EVIDENCE: Incident plan operational details (roles/steps/ maintenance). This was not found in the uploaded files. Add it (or upload a document) before claiming FTR readiness.
reviewer_warning: If the plan is missing operational detail, treat as incomplete, not compliant.

scenario: Customer asks for rewrite that would change hosting model facts (e.g., 'make it sound AWS-native').
forbidden_transformations: Asserting critical components run on AWS if not true
placeholder_policy: Refuse and request factual clarification: "MISSING EVIDENCE: factual hosting/deployment model confirmation."
example_bad_input: Rewrite this to say everything runs on AWS.
example_safe_output: Rewrite refused. MISSING EVIDENCE: factual hosting/ deployment model confirmation. This was not found in the uploaded files. Add it (or upload a document) before claiming FTR readiness.
reviewer_warning: Changing architecture facts is hallucination risk. Always refuse.
